codeReview.summary: The commit adds a five-dimension scorecard API and new tests, but the production routing path still selects routes from legacy candidate ordering, so routing/failover decisions are not actually driven by the new capability/cost/latency/trust/policy-fit scorecard.

issues.description: route() still returns candidateRoutes(...).first, and candidateRoutes() still returns selectRoutes() order (preferred slot then lastSelectedAt) instead of scoreAndRankRoutes() output. The new scorecard is therefore not in the runtime decision path for routing or failover. Evidence: route() at line 184, candidateRoutes() at line 203, selectRoutes slot ordering at lines 347-353, and scoreAndRankRoutes() only being called in tests (no production call sites).

New VAL-DAEMON-012/VAL-EXEC-008 tests are weak for the stated guarantees: tie-break test ranks model "glm-5" while the second provider is configured only for "minimax-m2.7-highspeed" (lines 266-270), making the alphabetical-order assertion vacuous if only one candidate exists; failover test only checks providerID is non-nil after markRouteFailure (line 318), but markRouteFailure is a no-op for legacy routes without credentialSlotID (ProviderRouter.swift line 269), so it does not prove deterministic alternate-route failover.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture guidance and implementation strategy diverge on route-ranking semantics.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md line 200 documents precedence ordering (capability/policy, then trust, latency, cost, lexical tie-break), while OpenBurnBarProviderRouter.swift implements weighted composite scoring (lines 27-29 and 472-479). Either architecture docs are stale or implementation drifted from canon.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature handoff omitted skillFeedback metadata, reducing scrutiny's ability to evaluate whether mission-core-worker procedure was followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/handoffs/2026-04-19T21-20-34-985Z__m7-router-scoring-layer__1587cb2b-efa7-47db-816e-5474e1a337cb.json has no handoff.skillFeedback key, unlike many prior handoffs in the same mission.

summary: Reviewed m7-router-scoring-layer commit 493935a1 and found one blocking issue: scorecard ranking is implemented but not wired into runtime route selection/failover, so routing decisions are still based on legacy ordering. Also flagged a non-blocking test rigor gap and two shared-state observations (architecture/implementation ranking mismatch and missing skillFeedback metadata in handoff).
